[2019-06-28 15:45] mrguy888: To answer his actual question, which is a situation that does come up semi regularly even if you do play well, just use your brain. What does the damage do next turn? What can you spend the 2 gold on? If you are doing 6 into eeww and the extra engi you buy saves you from having to buy a rhino next turn, then that seems pretty okay, but if you are doing 7 into eeww and the splitter forces a rhino buy, then that seems pretty good. 2g is what nivo costs so don't use it to mean 2 gold.

[2019-06-28 18:43] megasupp (mega-supp): And actually 1 damage next turn is worth slightly more than 2 gold in most sets. The reason being if you take the most efficient soak in the set and convert its cost into gold (R=1gold G=1.33gold B=1.67gold) you get that their gold/defence ratio (also called that unit's BV) is >2 (for example wall BV is 2.22). Exceptions are aegis BV=2 polywall BV=1.95 plexo cell BV= 1.92
[2019-06-28 18:46] megasupp (mega-supp): but that's in a vacuum. As mrguy said you need to look what exactly that point of damage forces your opp to buy
[2019-06-28 18:48] jamberine: isn't it a half turn behind though
[2019-06-28 18:55] megasupp (mega-supp): so then it is actually worth less then 2 gold
[2019-06-28 18:57] amalloy: if you're spending the 2 gold on an engineer instead of something prompt, isn't it the gold that's half a turn behind the attack?
[2019-06-28 18:57] jamberine: well yeah it depends on the soak
[2019-06-28 19:00] jamberine: my general rule for auride core (which basically affects attack next turn since that point of attack has been defended) is click it to deny absorb, make your turn more convenient, or hit granularity, and avoid clicking it when defense starts to become hard for your opponent

[2019-07-25 20:49] stacko: After starting with deadeyes, once you switch to regular attack you have to get over the absorb barrier to do damage again.

So, the first 4 attackers you got are just there to get past the energy matrix. And, for your first attackers, you often want to get slower attack so it can't be absorbed instantly.

Oxide mixers are nice first attackers because you don't have to click the pixies until they are actually doing damage. Tarsiers are also pretty good first attackers because they don't attack for a turn, letting synch up more attack at once.

Then, once you're past that, go for the fast attackers, hellhound and electrovore in this set.

Getting 3 blastforges and 2 animuses was probably not too great, sure you have a few turns of spending all your tech on hellhounds but after that you have trouble spending all the red.

Way back on turn 3, you bought DDDE, probably I would have picked up a conduit there instead of the engineer. Since it's a deadeye set you're probably going to buy out the drones eventually anyways, so it's not too important to buy them as fast as possible. (Though of course following the standard deadeye build is an even better idea)
[2019-07-25 20:55] velizar_ (Name): Yeah I overestimated the efficiency of Hellhound.

[2019-08-13 14:17] pflanzenesser (Julius): Quick Question. When you breach which units should you kill first? So far I always gone for the drones.
[2019-08-13 14:19] deadhour: Depends on the situation
[2019-08-13 14:20] deadhour: If opponent is heavy econ, low attack, then killing Drones seems fair because you're probably just fine soaking their damage for another turn
[2019-08-13 14:20] deadhour: If they're high attack, low econ, then you kill attackers since they're likely unable to rebuild
[2019-08-13 14:22] deadhour: But there's a lot of factors at play - how much you're breaching for, what attackers they're using, what are the defensive resources like
[2019-08-13 14:31] velizar_ (Name): I almost always prioritize tarsiers and other fragile units.
[2019-08-13 14:31] velizar_ (Name): One attack per turn is equivalent to two gold per turn. If your opponent has a 2 hp 1 attack unit, then killing it is about as good as killing two drones.
[2019-08-13 14:33] velizar_ (Name): Vivid drones give 3 gold per turn and are therefore worth one and a half tarsier. Two vivid drones vs a Cynestra are about equally good choices.
[2019-08-13 14:34] velizar_ (Name): With that in mind, it's also situational depending on e.g. whether you are going to be hit hard next turn, if you are then prioritize attackers more
[2019-08-13 14:36] velizar_ (Name): Wounding fragile units like [dynamo] is another dilemma
[2019-08-13 14:37] velizar_ (Name): https://gamepedia.cursecdn.com/prismata_gamepedia/b/be/ThoriumDynamo-panel.png
[2019-08-13 14:38] velizar_ (Name): It's not necessarily worth to wound it, because it is still doing the full amount of work until it is killed. It depends.
[2019-08-13 14:39] velizar_ (Name): But against breach proof, thorium dynamo is normally the highest priority target.
[2019-08-13 14:40] pflanzenesser (Julius): Never seen that one. But I only played a few matches, still very new to the game 😄
[2019-08-13 14:42] deadhour: Boy, do people love to complain about Thorium Dynamo
[2019-08-13 14:42] deadhour: You'll learn soon enough
